name: 🛒 Shop
description: E-commerce schema with LIVE 30-second ingestion: a new batch of orders lands every half-minute, silver refreshes every minute, and an hourly + sub-hourly gold view stay current. Demonstrates the incremental merge strategy at sub-minute cadence.

table_metadata.description: Customer master. Full refresh — the demo regenerates all 500 customers each run.

column_descriptions.email: Unique email — used as the natural key.
column_descriptions.name: Display name.
column_descriptions.country: ISO-like country code (FR / DE / NL / BE / IT / ES).
column_descriptions.signup_at: Date the customer first signed up.
column_descriptions.is_premium: Whether the customer is on a paid plan (~22%).

table_metadata.description: Product catalogue. 50 SKUs across audio / stickers / apparel / accessories / print.

pipelines.name: orders
pipelines.description: LIVE orders stream — initial 60-day backfill on first run, then 4 fresh orders every 30 seconds.

table_metadata.description: Live order facts — 5000 historical rows backfilled on first run, then ~4 new orders every 30 seconds. ordered_at is a TIMESTAMP, so 'last hour' / 'last 5 minutes' analyses work.

column_descriptions.order_id: Primary key. Backfill IDs are 1..5000; live IDs are epoch-based to guarantee uniqueness across runs.
column_descriptions.ordered_at: Order TIMESTAMP — also the watermark column. Backfill spans the last 60 days; live rows land in the last 25 seconds.

pipelines.name: orders_enriched
pipelines.description: Orders joined with customer + product, only completed orders kept — uses ref() across three bronze tables.

pipelines.name: revenue_by_month
pipelines.description: Monthly revenue + AOV — scheduled every 5 minutes so dashboards stay current.

pipelines.name: revenue_by_hour
pipelines.description: Hourly revenue + order count for the last 48 hours — the live-cadence counterpart of revenue_by_month.

table_metadata.description: Hourly revenue trend for the trailing 48 hours. Refreshed every minute so 'last hour' analyses are always current with the live bronze.orders stream.

column_descriptions.revenue_eur: Sum of revenue_eur in that hour.

pipelines.name: customer_segments
pipelines.description: PYTHON pipeline — buckets every customer into whale / loyal / casual via PyArrow.

table_metadata.description: Customer segments synthesised in Python (PyArrow Table from a list comprehension). Shows that pipelines aren't SQL-only.

column_descriptions.estimated_spend_eur: Heuristic spend estimate used by the segmentation.

quality_tests.name: orders_have_customer
quality_tests.description: Every order joined to the silver fact must have a matching customer.

schedules._comment: Every minute — keep the joined fact fresh.
